meta.description: Module 5 — Positioning & Crowd-Risk Engine. SAMPLE / INDICATIVE values shaped on public CFTC COT / ICE COT report formats. Update weekly after the Friday COT release. See docs/DAILY_UPDATE_GUIDE.md.
meta.methodology: Managed-money net positioning from the public CFTC Commitments of Traders and ICE COT reports, expressed in contracts and as a percentile of the 3-year range. Crowding is judged by a three-part checklist: (1) is positioning stretched, (2) is market structure confirming or weakening, (3) do physical flows support the position? Stretched positioning with weakening structure and neutral physical support = elevated liquidation risk. Positioning data is published with a lag (Tuesday data, Friday release) — treat as directional, not real-time.

positioning.note: Longs rebuilding with the inventory draws — positioning follows the physical story rather than leading it.
Fastest four-week build in a year. Length is concentrated in the front — sensitive to any prompt disappointment.
Near record short on the soft diesel story. Crowded consensus positions are fragile against supply surprises.
Middle of the range — the driving-season trade is on but not crowded.

checklist.detail: Managed money at the 82nd percentile of the 3-year range, +25k w/w.
M1/M2 still tightening (+0.10 w/w) — structure currently confirms the length.
Prompt physical supportive (stock draws), but Atlantic Basin length building at 2.3σ (Module 2) undermines the deferred story.

crowd_risk.explanation: Two of three crowding conditions are live. The length is stretched but still 'earned' while prompt spreads tighten. The trigger to watch: if Brent M1/M2 stalls or the WAF overhang starts landing, an 82nd-percentile long has a lot of company at the exit — liquidation risk steps up to elevated.
crowd_risk.watch: Brent M1/M2 momentum (Module 3); WAF differentials and floating storage (Module 2); next COT print for continued build.
crowd_risk.source: The Crude Oracle assessment on CFTC/ICE COT data

checklist.detail: 8th percentile of the 3-year range — near record short.
Diesel crack still loosening, but distillate stocks sit below seasonal average — the cushion is thinner than the positioning implies.
Rotterdam outage (Module 4, 400 kb/d) and tightening clean freight into Europe (Module 2) both point against the short.

crowd_risk.explanation: A crowded short into rising supply risk: the outage removes diesel production while stocks are already below seasonal norms. If the crack starts repricing, the 8th-percentile short covers into it — classic squeeze mechanics. This is the positioning backdrop to Hypothesis #1 in Module 6.
crowd_risk.watch: ARA distillate stocks (two consecutive weekly draws would be the tell); Rotterdam restart guidance; gasoil timespreads.
crowd_risk.source: The Crude Oracle assessment on ICE COT data

checklist.detail: M1/M2 tightening with Cushing draws — structure confirms.
Export pull and hub draws both support the position (Modules 1–2).

crowd_risk.explanation: No crowding conditions met: moderate length, confirming structure, supportive physical. The cleanest positioning picture on the board.
crowd_risk.watch: Cushing stocks approaching operational minimums can cause spread spikes — volatility risk, not crowding risk.
crowd_risk.source: The Crude Oracle assessment on CFTC COT data
